title: gilia tenuiflora subsp. tenuiflora
intro: Gilia tenuiflora subsp. tenuiflora, also known as Bear's Claw or Purple Columbine, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Gilia tenuiflora subsp. tenuiflora include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, treat respiratory issues, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
